gate_rows.name: typed carrier identity
required_preconditions: Domain-originated async work has a typed CapSched carrier distinct from raw work_struct
worker task identity is explicitly rejected as caller Domain authority
carrier lifetime is described from allocation through free
forbidden_fallbacks: worker current as caller authority
work_struct address as authority
callback function pointer as authority
generic queue membership as caller provenance
gate_rows.patch_precondition: patch proposal names the carrier object, ownership, lifetime, and retrieval path before touching worker execution

gate_rows.name: pending coalescing preservation
required_preconditions: queue_work false path preserves the first carrier caller identity
queue_work false path preserves the first carrier BudgetTicket
queue_work false path preserves the first monitor receipt and generation
second caller handling is explicit and cannot overwrite saved authority
forbidden_fallbacks: overwrite caller fields on an already-pending work_struct
merge second caller authority into first carrier without a model
charge second caller budget for work it did not own
treat pending bit as caller-specific provenance
gate_rows.patch_precondition: patch proposal includes a pending-coalescing table for already pending, canceled, executing, and requeued work

gate_rows.name: caller BudgetTicket ownership
required_preconditions: caller-owned BudgetTicket or explicitly split child ticket exists before enqueue
settlement and overrun behavior are defined before endpoint side effects
service-only budget is insufficient for caller-originated work
forbidden_fallbacks: service-only budget as caller budget
worker kthread runtime as caller ticket
cgroup CPU accounting as capability budget
scheduler runtime charge as monitor-enforced budget
gate_rows.patch_precondition: patch proposal names caller ticket derivation, charge point, refund/cancel path, and overrun behavior

gate_rows.name: service and caller intersection
required_preconditions: service authority is bound separately from caller frozen authority
effective authority is the intersection of service authority and caller frozen authority
endpoint side effects occur only after intersection succeeds
forbidden_fallbacks: service Domain allow result as full authority
caller credential snapshot as full authority
namespace membership as full authority
LSM allow result as frozen capability
gate_rows.patch_precondition: patch proposal includes an authority-intersection table before any side-effecting endpoint operation

gate_rows.name: monitor receipt provenance
required_preconditions: monitor-minted receipt reference is distinct from Linux shadow state
Linux carrier may hold opaque receipt references but cannot mint receipt authority
receipt generation and carrier generation are compared before consumption
forbidden_fallbacks: Linux helper return code as receipt
cached shadow as monitor receipt
timeout refresh as renewed receipt
pointer identity as receipt authority
gate_rows.patch_precondition: patch proposal separates monitor receipt identity, Linux shadow fields, and generation comparison fields

gate_rows.name: revoke and stale carrier rejection
required_preconditions: revoked pending carriers are rejected before worker execution
stale generation or epoch cannot reach endpoint side effects
cancel, retry, completion, and free ordering are defined
forbidden_fallbacks: worker receives stale carrier after revoke
local cleanup as monitor revoke completion
retry as renewed receipt
free path that races with worker carrier consumption
gate_rows.patch_precondition: patch proposal includes a revoke/cancel/retry/free state table and stale-carrier rejection path

gate_rows.name: workqueue patch boundary
required_preconditions: generic kernel-internal work remains excluded unless classified
Domain-originated work uses a typed carrier wrapper
workqueue source map exists before code review
forbidden_fallbacks: global workqueue hook treats all work as Domain-originated
kernel-internal work inherits arbitrary caller authority
work_struct pending state stores caller authority directly
gate_rows.patch_precondition: patch proposal includes a workqueue source map and an exclusion list for kernel-internal work

gate_rows.name: io_uring patch boundary
required_preconditions: io_uring requests carry typed provenance before worker execution
registered resources freeze authority at registration or explicit update
io-wq worker identity is not caller authority
forbidden_fallbacks: registered fd existence as frozen authority
io-wq worker current as caller Domain
completion success as receipt renewal
gate_rows.patch_precondition: patch proposal includes an io_uring source map for request, registered resource, worker, completion, cancel, and retry paths

gate_rows.name: evidence class split
required_preconditions: source-only, formal, compile-only, trace-only, monitor-backed runtime, and production protection evidence are separately labeled
non-claims remain attached to every pre-patch artifact
patch proposal names the evidence class it intends to produce
forbidden_fallbacks: TLC pass as monitor verification
source anchor as runtime coverage
inert type as behavior approval
trace plan as public ABI
compile success as production protection
gate_rows.patch_precondition: patch proposal includes evidence-class table and explicit non-claims

required_before_patch: DCASYNC-001 through DCASYNC-009 satisfied or carried as explicit blockers
carrier storage lifetime for allocation, ownership, enqueue, coalescing, execution, cancellation, revoke, retry, completion, and free
pending-coalescing table for queue_work false behavior
worker retrieval path that rejects worker identity as authority
caller BudgetTicket derivation, settlement, cancel/refund, and overrun behavior
monitor receipt versus Linux shadow field split
separate workqueue and io_uring source maps before code review
evidence-class table with non-claims for ABI, tracepoints, runtime coverage, monitor verification, behavior change, and production protection
